BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin household budget after the town mentioned the reappraisal might bump property tax. Before Colleen's year-end tax pass.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Take-home ~$2,900/mo against ~$1,730 of outflow leaves roughly $1,170 surplus toward savings and the heating-oil pre-buy. Revisit with Colleen once the year-end figures are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Property tax escrow rises to $410/mo after the reappraisal
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Outflow $1,732 + $30 escrow increase = $1,762. Take-home of $2,900 covers it with ~$1,138 left over.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Still comfortably within the monthly take-home.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - a furnace replacement winter pushes heating oil higher
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Colleen wanted to stress test a bad-winter year.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Even in a higher property-tax and heavy-oil year the household stays well within the ~$2,900 monthly take-home. The main watch-item is the seasonal heating-oil swing, which the savings cushion covers.
ActionItems: Wait for the town to confirm the reappraisal property tax figure
Revisit after year-end with Colleen for the proper tax draft
